query: Can you point me to a paper that discussed transformer-based sentence embeddings?

title.value: Grammatical information in BERT sentence embeddings as two-dimensional arrays

recommendation_reason.value: Sentence embeddings induced with various transformer architectures encode much semantic and syntactic information

supported_research_question.value: We investigate whether specific grammatical information can be accessed in these distributed representations.

method.value: a two-dimensional reshaping of these vectors allows various learning architectures to access such information.

data_or_scenario.value: data from a task developed to test rule-like generalizations

main_conclusion.value: current sentence embeddings contain information that is regularly distributed, and which can be captured when the embeddings are reshaped into higher dimensional arrays.

limitations.value: Sentence embeddings induced with various transformer architectures encode much semantic and syntactic information in a distributed manner in a one-dimensional array.

relevance_strength.value: our experiments on detecting subject-verb agreement yield several promising results

title.value: Syntax-guided Neural Module Distillation to Probe Compositionality in Sentence Embeddings

recommendation_reason.value: Given a sentence, we construct a neural module net based on its syntax parse and train it end-to-end to approximate the sentence's embedding generated by a transformer model.

supported_research_question.value: Past work probing compositionality in sentence embedding models faces issues determining the causal impact of implicit syntax representations.

method.value: Given a sentence, we construct a neural module net based on its syntax parse and train it end-to-end to approximate the sentence's embedding generated by a transformer model.

data_or_scenario.value: We find differences in the distillability of various sentence embedding models

main_conclusion.value: We find differences in the distillability of various sentence embedding models that broadly correlate with their performance, but observe that distillability doesn't considerably vary by model size. We also present preliminary evidence that much syntax-guided composition in sentence embedding models is linear, and that non-linearities may serve primarily to handle non-compositional phrases.

limitations.value: We find differences in the distillability of various sentence embedding models that broadly correlate with their performance, but observe that distillability doesn't considerably vary by model size.

relevance_strength.value: Past work probing compositionality in sentence embedding models faces issues determining the causal impact of implicit syntax representations.

title.value: What you can cram into a single $&!#* vector: Probing sentence embeddings for linguistic properties

recommendation_reason.value: Although much effort has recently been devoted to training high-quality sentence embeddings, we still have a poor understanding of what they are capturing.

supported_research_question.value: Although much effort has recently been devoted to training high-quality sentence embeddings, we still have a poor understanding of what they are capturing.

method.value: Although much effort has recently been devoted to training high-quality sentence embeddings, we still have a poor understanding of what they are capturing.

data_or_scenario.value: "Downstream" tasks, often based on sentence classification, are commonly used to evaluate the quality of sentence representations.

main_conclusion.value: #* vector, sentence embedding methods have achieved impressive results in tasks ranging from machine translation (Sutskever et al.

limitations.value: Although much effort has recently been devoted to training high-quality sentence embeddings, we still have a poor understanding of what they are capturing.

relevance_strength.value: Although much effort has recently been devoted to training high-quality sentence embeddings, we still have a poor understanding of what they are capturing.
